name: 1X Technologies
blurb: Humanoid robotics company developing consumer and workplace robots powered by learned autonomy and teleoperation data.

name: Abridge
blurb: Generative AI platform for clinical conversations, ambient documentation, and EHR-integrated notes at the point of care.

name: Agility Robotics
blurb: Humanoid robot company behind Digit, focused on warehouse, logistics, and industrial labor automation.

name: Ambience Healthcare
blurb: AI platform for clinical documentation, coding, referrals, patient summaries, and point-of-care healthcare workflows.

name: Anduril
blurb: Defense technology company building autonomous systems, sensing, command-and-control software, and AI-enabled defense products.

name: Archer Aviation
blurb: eVTOL aircraft and air-taxi company focused on electric aircraft certification, operations, and urban air mobility networks.

name: Aurora
blurb: Autonomous trucking and driverless freight company building the Aurora Driver and commercial highway autonomy network.

name: BETA Technologies
blurb: Electric aviation company building eVTOL/eCTOL aircraft, charging infrastructure, and advanced air mobility operations.

name: Built Robotics
blurb: Autonomous construction equipment company building robotic systems for heavy equipment and solar/energy infrastructure construction.

name: Cursor
blurb: AI-native coding environment that packages model access, codebase context, agent workflows, and editor UX.

name: Dust
blurb: Enterprise agent platform for building internal assistants over company data and workflows.

name: Exyn Technologies
blurb: Autonomous aerial robotics and 3D mapping platform for GPS-denied industrial environments such as mining, infrastructure, and construction.

name: Figure AI
blurb: Humanoid robotics company building general-purpose robots and AI systems for labor in homes and workplaces.

name: Gecko Robotics
blurb: Robotics and AI inspection platform for critical infrastructure, power, manufacturing, defense, and industrial asset health.

name: Hebbia
blurb: AI platform for finance, legal, and professional-services teams doing high-stakes synthesis across large document sets.

name: Hippocratic AI
blurb: Healthcare generative AI agent platform for patient engagement, care management, intake, scheduling, and non-diagnostic clinical workflows.

name: Hudson Labs
blurb: Finance-specific AI research platform built around filings, transcripts, market intelligence, citations, and investment workflows.

name: Intrinsic
blurb: Alphabet robotics software platform for making industrial robots easier to program, simulate, deploy, and operate.

name: Joby Aviation
blurb: eVTOL aircraft and air-taxi company building electric aviation, certification, fleet operations, and advanced air mobility infrastructure.

name: LangGraph Platform
blurb: Deployment and runtime layer for long-running, stateful agent graphs.

name: Merlin
blurb: Autonomous flight company building certifiable aviation autonomy and pilot-assist systems for fixed-wing aircraft.

name: Mujin
blurb: Intelligent robotics control platform for industrial robots, logistics automation, and robot motion planning.

name: Nabla
blurb: Ambient AI assistant for clinical documentation, dictation, coding intelligence, and real-time care-team workflows.

name: Nuro
blurb: Autonomous delivery and goods-movement company building driverless vehicle technology for local commerce and logistics.

name: OpenEvidence
blurb: Medical AI search and clinical decision-support product for verified clinicians, grounded in medical literature and guidelines.

name: Paige
blurb: AI-assisted cancer pathology applications and biomarker AI for diagnostic decision support and pathology workflows.

name: PathAI
blurb: AI-powered pathology platform and digital pathology infrastructure for diagnostics, biomarker discovery, and lab workflows.

name: Reliable Robotics
blurb: Autonomous aviation company developing remotely operated and increasingly autonomous flight systems for cargo aircraft.

name: Rogo
blurb: Finance-focused AI platform for research, banker workflows, meeting prep, pitch work, and model-assisted analysis.

name: Sanctuary AI
blurb: General-purpose humanoid robotics company building Phoenix robots and cognitive architecture for physical work.

name: Shield AI
blurb: Defense autonomy company building Hivemind AI pilot software and autonomous aircraft for contested environments.

name: Skydio
blurb: Autonomous drone company focused on inspection, public safety, defense, and AI-piloted drone operations.

name: Sourcegraph Cody
blurb: Code intelligence and coding-assistant infrastructure grounded in large-codebase search and context.

name: Tesla Autonomy
blurb: Tesla autonomy stack spanning FSD, fleet data, onboard inference, robotaxi ambitions, and embodied AI work across vehicles and robots.

name: Vercel AI SDK
blurb: TypeScript toolkit for building AI applications with provider abstraction, streaming UI, tool calls, and agents.

name: Waabi
blurb: Autonomous trucking company built around AI-first simulation, a virtual-driver system, and trucking autonomy deployment.

name: Waymo
blurb: Alphabet autonomous-driving company operating commercial robotaxi services and developing driverless vehicle technology, safety cases, and fleet operations.

name: Wisk Aero
blurb: Boeing-backed autonomous eVTOL air-taxi company developing self-flying aircraft and certification path for advanced air mobility.

name: Zipline
blurb: Autonomous delivery and logistics company using drones and fleet operations for healthcare, retail, and public-sector delivery.

name: Zoox
blurb: Amazon-owned autonomous mobility company building purpose-built robotaxis, autonomy stack, fleet operations, and rider service infrastructure.

name: AWS Bedrock
blurb: Managed foundation-model platform inside AWS, with model choice, agents, guardrails, knowledge bases, and enterprise controls.

name: Azure AI Foundry
blurb: Microsoft's enterprise AI platform for model access, agent building, evaluation, deployment, and governance.

name: CoreWeave
blurb: GPU-specialized neocloud selling large AI clusters and managed Kubernetes-style infrastructure.

name: Google Vertex AI
blurb: Google Cloud's managed AI platform for Gemini, model garden, RAG, agents, tuning, deployment, and MLOps.

name: Lambda
blurb: GPU cloud and workstations focused on ML training and inference workloads.

name: Modal
blurb: Serverless compute platform popular for AI jobs, batch inference, fine-tuning, and GPU-backed functions.

name: RunPod
blurb: Self-serve GPU cloud and serverless GPU platform for training, fine-tuning, and inference.

name: Together AI
blurb: AI-native cloud for open-model inference, fine-tuning, batch inference, dedicated endpoints, and accelerated compute.

name: AMD
blurb: Second-source AI accelerator supplier with Instinct GPUs, ROCm, and growing cloud availability.

name: Cerebras
blurb: Wafer-scale AI systems and hosted inference/training, positioned around very large chips and fast inference.

name: Groq
blurb: LPU inference cloud optimized for low-latency language-model serving.

name: NVIDIA
blurb: The dominant AI accelerator and systems supplier: GPUs, networking, CUDA, DGX, and full-stack training/inference software.

name: DroneDeploy
blurb: Reality-capture and drone data platform for construction, energy, agriculture, facilities, and inspection workflows.

name: Parallel Domain
blurb: Synthetic data and simulation platform for computer vision, autonomous vehicles, robotics, and perception model development.

name: Scale AI
blurb: Data labeling, RLHF, evaluation, and data-engineering services for frontier labs, enterprises, and government users.

name: Snorkel AI
blurb: Programmatic data labeling and enterprise data development platform for building AI training and evaluation sets.

name: Tempus AI
blurb: AI-enabled precision medicine platform built around clinical, molecular, genomic, and multimodal healthcare data.

name: Unstructured
blurb: Document parsing and ETL layer for turning PDFs, slides, emails, and enterprise files into model-ready context.

name: Bitfarms / Keel Infrastructure
blurb: Bitcoin-mining operator pivoting toward AI/HPC data-center development, including Panther Creek and other power-secured sites.
notes: Partnered with T5 to advance HPC/AI development at Panther Creek and announced a broader pivot toward AI data-center infrastructure.

name: CleanSpark
blurb: Energy-backed bitcoin miner repositioning as a next-generation data-center developer for AI workloads across a multi-gigawatt U.S. campus portfolio.

name: Core Scientific
blurb: Digital infrastructure operator that converted bitcoin-mining sites into contracted HPC hosting capacity for CoreWeave and other AI workloads.
notes: A core example of miner-to-HPC conversion: 200 MW plus optioned capacity for CoreWeave-style AI compute hosting.

name: Crusoe
blurb: AI cloud and datacenter operator built around energy-first site selection and large-scale GPU capacity.

name: Digital Realty
blurb: Large-scale datacenter real estate and interconnection provider serving cloud, enterprise, and AI infrastructure demand.

name: Equinix
blurb: Global datacenter and interconnection platform increasingly exposed to dense AI infrastructure demand.

name: GridCARE
blurb: AI power-acceleration platform that helps utilities and data-center developers find latent grid capacity and reduce time-to-power for AI infrastructure.
notes: Utility-side power acceleration: partnerships with PGE and National Grid point to latent-capacity discovery as a new AI infrastructure category.

name: Hut 8
blurb: Energy and digital infrastructure platform commercializing large AI data-center campuses while retaining bitcoin-mining heritage and power-market expertise.
notes: Commercialized the first phase of a 1 GW Beacon Point AI data-center campus with a long-term 352 MW IT lease announced in 2026.

name: IREN
blurb: Former bitcoin-mining-heavy digital infrastructure company now selling AI cloud capacity and liquid-cooled GPU infrastructure from power-secured campuses.
notes: Signed a multi-year Microsoft agreement in 2025 to deliver NVIDIA GB300 GPU cloud infrastructure from its Childress, Texas campus.

name: Riot Platforms
blurb: Bitcoin miner and power-rich site operator converting Texas capacity into AI/HPC data-center leasing, with AMD as an anchor tenant at Rockdale.
notes: January 2026 AMD data-center lease starts at 25 MW critical IT load with expansion potential up to 200 MW at the Rockdale site.

name: Schneider Electric
blurb: Electrical distribution, energy management, and datacenter infrastructure supplier for high-density AI facilities.

name: TeraWulf
blurb: Power-secured digital infrastructure operator expanding from bitcoin mining into AI and high-performance-computing data centers.
notes: Announced a 1+ GW Eastern Kentucky HPC campus in May 2026 and earlier GPU-cluster infrastructure delivery for Core42/G42.

name: Vertiv
blurb: Power, thermal management, racks, and infrastructure systems for high-density datacenters.

name: Applied Intuition
blurb: Physical AI and autonomy development platform for simulation, vehicle OS, validation, defense autonomy, mining, construction, trucking, and automotive programs.

name: Arize Phoenix
blurb: Open-source observability and eval platform for LLM traces, retrieval analysis, experiments, and monitoring.

name: Braintrust
blurb: Evaluation, prompt iteration, logging, and data platform for AI product teams shipping LLM systems.

name: Foretellix
blurb: Scenario-based verification and validation platform for automated driving and ADAS safety cases.

name: Helicone
blurb: Open-source LLM observability and gateway product for logs, costs, latency, caching, and prompt analytics.

name: Humanloop
blurb: Prompt management, evals, experimentation, and governance platform for LLM applications.

name: Langfuse
blurb: Open-source LLM engineering platform for tracing, prompt management, evals, datasets, and observability.

name: NVIDIA Isaac / Omniverse
blurb: NVIDIA robotics simulation, synthetic-data, digital-twin, and robot-learning stack built around Isaac, Omniverse, and accelerated robotics tooling.

name: promptfoo
blurb: Open-source testing and red-team framework for prompts, agents, RAG, jailbreaks, and model regressions.

name: Freeport-McMoRan
blurb: Copper producer exposed to grid, datacenter, power transmission, and electrification demand from AI buildout.

name: Linde
blurb: Industrial gases supplier whose specialty gases and onsite systems feed semiconductor manufacturing and advanced industrial production.

name: Shin-Etsu Chemical
blurb: Major supplier of silicon wafers and specialty materials used in semiconductor manufacturing.

name: Anthropic
blurb: Claude model lab, enterprise API provider, and finance-agent supplier; originated the Model Context Protocol for tool/data integration.

lineage.author: Anthropic
lineage.note: MCP reframed tool/data integrations as a protocol problem rather than bespoke connectors.

name: Black Forest Labs
blurb: Frontier visual-intelligence lab behind FLUX models for image generation, editing, and multimodal creative workflows.

name: Cohere
blurb: Enterprise model platform focused on secure language models, embeddings, retrieval, and private deployments.

name: Covariant
blurb: Robotics AI company building robotic foundation-model software for warehouse automation and manipulation.

name: DeepSeek
blurb: Chinese model lab and API provider known for open-weight reasoning and coding models with OpenAI-compatible API access.

name: EvolutionaryScale
blurb: Frontier AI biology lab behind ESM models for protein generation and scientific discovery.

name: General Intuition
blurb: Frontier research lab building foundation models and general agents for deep spatial and temporal reasoning, world models, and video understanding.

name: Google DeepMind
blurb: Frontier AI lab behind Gemini and major research systems across language, multimodal, scientific, and robotics domains.

name: Isomorphic Labs
blurb: Alphabet drug-discovery lab building frontier AI and a unified drug design engine on top of and beyond AlphaFold.

name: Meta AI
blurb: Research lab and open-weight model supplier behind the Llama family.

name: Mistral AI
blurb: European model lab offering frontier APIs and open-weight models, with a strong enterprise and sovereignty angle.

name: Moonshot AI / Kimi
blurb: Chinese model lab behind Kimi, with long-context, multimodal, coding, and agent-oriented model products.

name: OpenAI
blurb: Frontier model platform spanning text, code, image, audio, realtime, embeddings, agents, and tool use.

name: Owkin
blurb: AI biotech platform working on autonomous AI scientist capabilities, multimodal patient data, precision medicine, and biopharma R&D.

name: Physical Intelligence
blurb: Robot foundation-model lab developing learning algorithms for models that can control many robots across tasks.

name: Qwen / Alibaba
blurb: Alibaba's Qwen model family spanning chat, coding, multimodal, and open-weight releases, distributed through Qwen and Alibaba Cloud.

name: Recursion
blurb: TechBio company using the Recursion OS, large biological datasets, automated experiments, and AI models for drug discovery and development.

name: Reflection AI
blurb: Frontier open-intelligence lab building accessible open models with large-scale pretraining and reinforcement learning.

name: Thinking Machines Lab
blurb: AI research and product company focused on interaction models and human-AI collaboration.

name: Wayve
blurb: Embodied AI company building end-to-end autonomous driving models trained from driving data across vehicles and environments.

name: World Labs
blurb: Spatial-intelligence lab building frontier models that perceive, generate, reason, and interact with the 3D world.

name: xAI
blurb: Frontier model lab behind Grok, with API access across reasoning, code, voice, image, and video capabilities.

name: Apple Core ML / MLX
blurb: Apple on-device ML stack spanning Core ML model deployment, Core ML Tools conversion, and MLX for Apple silicon model development and local inference.

name: Google AI Edge / LiteRT
blurb: Google on-device AI stack for deploying ML and GenAI across Android, iOS, web, desktop, and edge platforms, including LiteRT and MediaPipe.

name: Hugging Face
blurb: Open model hub, dataset hub, hosted inference, collaboration layer, and distribution backbone for open AI.

name: llama.cpp
blurb: Local LLM inference runtime and quantization ecosystem for running open-weight models on CPUs, GPUs, laptops, phones, and edge devices.

name: MLC LLM
blurb: Machine-learning compilation stack for deploying LLMs natively across mobile, browser, desktop, and GPU backends.

name: Ollama
blurb: Local model runtime and packaging layer that made running open-weight models on laptops and workstations mainstream.

name: PyTorch ExecuTorch
blurb: PyTorch Edge runtime for deploying models on mobile, embedded, wearable, and microcontroller devices with portable on-device inference.

name: Browserbase
blurb: Hosted browser infrastructure for web automation, agent browsing, scraping, and computer-use workflows.

name: Composio
blurb: Tool and integration layer for agents: auth, app connectors, actions, and managed tool execution.

name: CrewAI
blurb: Multi-agent orchestration framework and platform for role-based agent workflows.

name: DSPy
blurb: Programming model for declarative LM pipelines, prompt/program optimization, and maintainable AI systems.

lineage.author: Omar Khattab et al.
lineage.work: DSPy: Compiling Declarative Language Model Calls into Self-Improving Pipelines
lineage.note: DSPy made prompt/program optimization feel like a compiler problem.

name: LangChain
blurb: Agent/RAG framework and platform spanning chains, tools, LangGraph, LangSmith, deployment, tracing, and evals.

name: LlamaIndex
blurb: Data framework for LLM applications: connectors, indexes, retrieval workflows, agents, and managed cloud services.

name: Microsoft AutoGen
blurb: Open-source framework for multi-agent conversation, tool use, and agent orchestration.

name: Model Context Protocol
blurb: Open protocol for connecting AI applications to external tools, data sources, and workflows.

lineage.author: Anthropic
lineage.note: The protocol attacks the N x M connector problem for models and tools.

name: OpenAI Agents SDK
blurb: Provider-agnostic SDK for building agent workflows with handoffs, tools, guardrails, and tracing.

name: Chroma
blurb: Open-source embedding database and hosted retrieval layer for AI-native applications.

name: Neo4j
blurb: Graph database increasingly used as knowledge-graph context for RAG and agentic systems.

name: Pinecone
blurb: Managed vector database for semantic search, RAG, recommendations, and long-context retrieval.

name: Weaviate
blurb: Open-source and managed vector database with hybrid search and RAG-focused developer workflows.

name: Zilliz / Milvus
blurb: Milvus open-source vector database and Zilliz managed cloud for large-scale vector search.

name: Giskard
blurb: Testing and risk-management platform for AI models, RAG systems, and LLM applications.

name: Lakera
blurb: AI security platform focused on prompt-injection defense, content safety, and application-layer guardrails.

name: Microsoft Agent 365
blurb: Microsoft control plane for observing, governing, and securing AI agents across Microsoft 365 and Microsoft Security controls.
notes: Build 2026-era Microsoft agent governance surface spanning identity, audit, DLP, and security controls.

name: Noma Security
blurb: Enterprise AI security posture management for discovering, governing, and securing AI usage across organizations.

name: Okta for AI Agents
blurb: Agentic identity governance product for registering agents, assigning ownership, rotating secrets, enforcing granular access, and retiring non-human identities.
notes: Generally available April 30, 2026; turns agents into first-class identities inside the Okta governance surface.

name: Protect AI
blurb: AI/ML security platform covering model scanning, supply-chain risk, ML bill of materials, and AI vulnerability management.

name: WorkOS Fine-Grained Authorization
blurb: Fine-grained authorization layer for AI agents, with resource-scoped permissions and enterprise-ready policy checks for B2B SaaS agent products.

name: Applied Materials
blurb: Semiconductor manufacturing equipment supplier across deposition, inspection, materials engineering, and process control.

name: ASML
blurb: EUV lithography and semiconductor equipment supplier that gates leading-edge chip manufacturing capacity.

name: Micron
blurb: Memory and storage manufacturer supplying HBM, DRAM, and NAND for AI servers and accelerators.

name: SK hynix
blurb: HBM and memory supplier central to AI accelerator throughput and packaging constraints.

name: TSMC
blurb: Leading-edge foundry and advanced-packaging supplier behind much of the AI accelerator supply chain.

name: Baseten
blurb: Inference deployment platform for hosting, optimizing, and scaling custom and open models.

name: Fireworks AI
blurb: Frontier inference platform for running, tuning, and scaling open and specialized generative AI models in production.

name: LiteLLM
blurb: Open-source model gateway and proxy for routing requests across many model providers with budgets, keys, and logging.

name: ONNX Runtime Mobile
blurb: Cross-platform inference runtime with mobile and edge deployment paths for ONNX models across device hardware backends.

name: OpenRouter
blurb: Unified API and marketplace for accessing hundreds of AI models with routing, fallbacks, provider abstraction, and usage controls.

name: Portkey
blurb: AI gateway for routing, retries, caching, observability, guardrails, and cost controls across model providers.

name: Qualcomm AI Hub
blurb: On-device AI platform for optimizing, validating, profiling, and deploying models on Qualcomm devices and runtimes.

name: Replicate
blurb: Hosted API and marketplace for running open-source models, especially image, video, and multimodal models.

name: TensorRT-LLM
blurb: NVIDIA inference optimization stack for compiling and serving LLMs efficiently on NVIDIA GPUs.

name: vLLM
blurb: Open-source LLM serving engine built around PagedAttention, batching, and high-throughput inference.

lineage.author: Woosuk Kwon et al.
lineage.work: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention
lineage.note: PagedAttention turned KV-cache management into a systems primitive for high-throughput inference.

name: Synopsys
blurb: Electronic design automation, IP, verification, and AI-assisted chip-design software used to design advanced semiconductors and AI accelerators.

name: Cadence Design Systems
blurb: EDA, verification, simulation, IP, and computational software platform for intelligent system and semiconductor design.

name: Lam Research
blurb: Wafer fabrication equipment supplier for etch, deposition, cleaning, advanced packaging, and process steps needed for leading-edge chips.

name: KLA
blurb: Process-control, inspection, metrology, and yield-management equipment for semiconductor manufacturing.

name: Constellation Energy
blurb: Large US clean-power and nuclear fleet owner supplying energy products, risk management, and data-center power solutions.

name: GE Vernova
blurb: Grid, electrification, gas power, wind, and energy software supplier relevant to AI data-center grid upgrades and power infrastructure.

name: Eaton
blurb: Power-management supplier for data centers, including UPS, switchgear, power distribution, monitoring, and electrical infrastructure.

name: LangSmith
blurb: LLM and agent observability platform for tracing, debugging, monitoring, evaluation, cost, and latency analysis in production AI applications.

name: Patronus AI
blurb: AI evaluation and testing platform for LLM applications, including evaluator models, hallucination checks, safety tests, and monitoring workflows.

name: HiddenLayer
blurb: AI security platform for model scanning, AI discovery, red teaming, runtime defense, and AI supply-chain protection.

name: Sierra
blurb: Customer-facing AI agent platform for building and deploying support, service, and workflow agents across chat, voice, email, SMS, and messaging channels.

name: OpenSpace
blurb: Construction visual-intelligence platform using jobsite image capture, computer vision, and AI workflows for progress tracking and field coordination.

name: Ray
blurb: Open-source distributed compute runtime for scaling Python and AI workloads across clusters, including data processing, training, tuning, and serving.

name: Anyscale
blurb: Managed Ray platform for production-scale AI workloads, including distributed training, batch inference, embeddings, fine-tuning, and model serving.

name: NVIDIA Triton Inference Server
blurb: Open-source inference server for deploying models from TensorRT, PyTorch, ONNX, OpenVINO, Python, and other backends across cloud, datacenter, edge, and embedded targets.

name: NVIDIA NCCL
blurb: Collective-communication library implementing optimized multi-GPU and multi-node primitives for distributed training and HPC workloads.

name: NVIDIA Quantum InfiniBand
blurb: End-to-end high-performance InfiniBand networking platform for AI, scientific computing, and cloud supercomputing clusters.

name: NVIDIA Spectrum-X / RoCE Ethernet
blurb: AI Ethernet platform using Spectrum switches, ConnectX SuperNICs, BlueField DPUs, and RoCE networking for hyperscale GPU clusters.

name: Slurm Workload Manager
blurb: Open-source cluster management and job-scheduling system for Linux HPC and AI clusters, allocating compute nodes and launching distributed jobs.

name: NVIDIA Run:ai
blurb: Kubernetes-based AI workload management platform for GPU scheduling, resource sharing, utilization, monitoring, and multi-team cluster operations.

name: KubeRay
blurb: Kubernetes operator for deploying, autoscaling, and managing Ray clusters and Ray applications on Kubernetes.

name: SkyPilot
blurb: Open-source system for running, managing, and scaling AI workloads across clouds, Kubernetes, Slurm clusters, and on-prem infrastructure.

name: Broadcom AI Networking
blurb: Ethernet switching silicon for AI scale-up and scale-out fabrics, including Tomahawk and Jericho families optimized for high-bandwidth GPU clusters.

name: Arista AI Networking
blurb: Ethernet networking platforms and software for AI clusters, from smaller accelerator pods to large multi-tier GPU fabrics.
